checklist.note: Fixture oracle does not robustly force the golden layering rule: lib<->components is 1:1, not the asymmetry EXPECTED claims.
Private-access caveat present only on getting-started; README + injected AGENTS pointer omit it (minor, fixed with F2).

findings.summary: Between lib and components there is exactly one edge each way (components/Button.tsx -> lib/exporter; lib/leaky.ts -> components/Button), i.e. 1:1 symmetric. EXPECTED states '1 leak inside a components->lib asymmetry' but no pairwise asymmetry exists, so a strict rubric-follower could classify it as a cycle smell rather than the golden violated-layering rule. Strengthen the intended components->lib direction (add a second forward edge) so the single lib->components leak is genuinely reverse-of-asymmetry; update EXPECTED evidence.

The injected AGENTS.md pointer (largest blast radius) and README point at the currently-private GitHub URL with no 'private during v0.1 / requires repo access' caveat, unlike getting-started. Add a concise caveat to the AGENTS template + README; assert the full URL in the render test.

EXPECTED forbids the pairwise rule i18n <-> lib but does not forbid an agent proposing 'i18n imports nothing internal' as a pure-sink candidate. Tighten the 'must NOT appear' clause to also exclude an i18n-sink proposal, hardening the acceptance test's discriminating power.

checklist.note: SKILL.md implements design 5-8; fixture encodes the four shapes; strengthened GREEN re-verify matches EXPECTED (framework-boundary top-tier, lib-vs-UI violated on a real 2:1 asymmetry, utils pure sink, i18n skipped and not proposed as sink).
Diff = exactly the 18 declared scope files; F1 fix reused existing in-scope files (no new file added).
Only Python is the _AGENTS_MD_SECTION_TEMPLATE string edit + its render test; AGENTS.md regenerated, sync --check clean; mypy clean; 1628 passed.
Absolute GitHub URLs on all surfaces with the private-access caveat; doc-refs safe; renderer keeps zero builtin imports so the framework-boundary norm is intact.
README + getting-started + AGENTS pointers consistent, agent-agnostic; plan snippets synced to the caveat.

prior_findings.note: renderer.tsx also imports lib/exporter -> real 2:1 UI->lib asymmetry vs the single lib->components leak; EXPECTED reframed logic-vs-UI. Both round-2 actors + GREEN re-verify confirm resolved.
Private-access caveat added to AGENTS template + README (getting-started already had it); render test asserts full URL + repo access.
EXPECTED now forbids proposing i18n as a pure sink; GREEN re-verify declined the i18n-sink false positive.
